easy.q: In which state is the Grand Canyon National Park located?

Which national park contains the tallest waterfall in North America, Yosemite Falls?
Which national park is famous for its massive granite cliff called El Capitan?
In which state is Yellowstone National Park primarily located?
Which Florida national park is the only subtropical wilderness in North America?
What is the name of the famous geyser basin found in Yellowstone?
Which national park in Utah is known for its striking red-rock arches, including Delicate Arch?
Which national park sits on the border of Tennessee and North Carolina?
What is the most visited national park in the United States?
Which national park in Alaska contains Denali, the highest peak in North America?
Which national park in California is home to ancient giant sequoia trees?
Which national park preserves the largest coral reef system in the continental United States?
In which state is Acadia National Park located?
Which national park in South Dakota features the carved faces of four US presidents?
What large lake sits at the heart of Crater Lake National Park?
Which national park in Utah has a famous Zion Canyon carved by the Virgin River?
In which state is the Everglades National Park located?
Which animal is the symbol of Yellowstone and roams its plains in large herds?
Which national park is located on a volcanic island chain in Hawaii?
Which national park along the Colorado River is sometimes called 'The Maze'?
Which park in Montana is known as the 'Crown of the Continent'?
What large predator was reintroduced to Yellowstone in 1995?
Which national park in Oregon contains the deepest lake in the United States?
Which national park in Virginia is threaded end-to-end by Skyline Drive and the Appalachian Trail?
What type of tree gives Redwood National Park its name?
Which desert national park in California is known for its twisted Joshua trees?
In which state is Rocky Mountain National Park located?
Which national park protects the famous Mammoth Cave system?
What is the name of the iconic arch in Arches National Park most often used as its symbol?
Which national park in Utah is famous for its towering hoodoo rock formations?
In which state is Shenandoah National Park located?
Which national park straddles the US–Canada border in Montana and Alberta?
What large marine animal is commonly spotted in Olympic National Park's tidal pools?
Which national park in Washington state features the Hoh Rain Forest?
What color are the famous hot springs pools at Yellowstone's Grand Prismatic Spring?
easy.a: Bright rainbow colors (blue, green, yellow, orange, red)

easy.q: Which national park in Wyoming is famous for its dramatic jagged peaks reflected in the Snake River?
Which national park is home to the Valley of Ten Thousand Smokes in Alaska?
In which state is Saguaro National Park, named for its giant cactus, located?
Which national park preserves a large petrified wood forest in Arizona?
What large bird, once nearly extinct, has been reintroduced at Grand Canyon and Zion?
Which national park in New Mexico preserves stunning white gypsum sand dunes?
What is the name of the highest paved road in Rocky Mountain National Park?
Which national park is known for Mesa Verde's ancient cliff dwellings?
In which state is the Badlands National Park located?
What is the name of the large island that contains Katmai National Park?
Which type of bear is commonly photographed catching salmon at Katmai National Park?
Which national park encompasses both temperate rain forest and glaciated peaks in Washington?
What river carved the Grand Canyon over millions of years?
Which national park in Virginia and West Virginia includes a stretch of the Appalachian Trail along a ridge?
Which large national park in Alaska is roughly the size of West Virginia?
What iconic trail runs through Zion National Park along a narrow riverside slot canyon?
In which state is Congaree National Park, protecting old-growth bottomland forest, located?
What is the name of the scenic drive in Acadia National Park that passes the summit of Cadillac Mountain?
Which national park in Texas features the Chisos Mountains and borders the Rio Grande?

medium.q: Which national park was the first established in the United States, created in 1872?
Which president signed the act establishing Yellowstone as the world's first national park?
What is the name of the iconic rock formation in Wyoming that rises 867 feet above the surrounding terrain and appears in 'Close Encounters of the Third Kind'?
Which national park contains the largest glacier in the contiguous United States?
What is the name of the famous half-dome rock in Yosemite National Park?
Which national park in the Virgin Islands protects coral reefs and white-sand beaches?
The Going-to-the-Sun Road crosses the Continental Divide through which national park?
Which national park protects the world's longest known cave system?
What term describes the colorful, mineral-stained terraces formed by hot springs at Yellowstone's Mammoth Hot Springs?
Which national park in Nevada contains Lehman Caves and the Snake Range's Wheeler Peak?
In what year did the National Park Service officially form?
Which national park in North Dakota preserves the badlands where Theodore Roosevelt ranched?
What is the name of the waterfall in Yosemite that drops 2,425 feet and is the tallest in North America?
Which national park in Washington state includes the active stratovolcano Mount Rainier?
What is the deepest lake in the United States, found in Crater Lake National Park?
Which national park straddles the border of Arizona and Utah and is known for its slot canyons and the Wave formation?
medium.a: Grand Staircase-Escalante (monument, not park) — Vermilion Cliffs area

medium.q: What type of geological feature is a hoodoo, famously found in Bryce Canyon?
medium.a: A tall, thin spire of rock eroded from sedimentary rock

medium.q: Which national park in California protects both marine and terrestrial life on the Channel Islands?
The Angel's Landing trail with its chains section is a famous hike in which national park?
Which national park in Michigan consists of four islands in Lake Superior?
What is the name of the glacier that is retreating rapidly in Glacier National Park and is often cited in climate discussions?
Which national park in Minnesota protects a vast wilderness of lakes and forests near the Canadian border?
What massive lava tube in Hawaiʻi Volcanoes National Park can visitors walk through?
Which national park protects the ancient Anasazi cliff dwellings at Cliff Palace?
What is the name of Denali's highest point, officially measured at 20,310 feet?
Which national park in the Mojave Desert features a salt flat called the Devil's Golf Course?
What is the lowest point in North America, found in Death Valley National Park?
medium.a: Badwater Basin (282 feet below sea level)

medium.q: Which national park in Colorado preserves massive sand dunes at the base of the Sangre de Cristo Mountains?
What is the name of the volcanic peak in Oregon whose eruption in 1980 devastated the surrounding landscape?
The Bright Angel Trail descends from the South Rim into which national park's canyon?
Which national park in South Carolina protects one of the largest old-growth bottomland forests in the eastern United States?
What nickname describes the Everglades, coined by naturalist Marjory Stoneman Douglas, that captures its character as a wide, slow-moving sheet of water?
Which national park near Jackson Hole, Wyoming features the Teton mountain range?
What volcano in Hawaiʻi Volcanoes National Park has been erupting nearly continuously since 1983?
Which national park in Arkansas is famous for its hot springs and historic bathhouses?
What is the name of the backcountry permit system required for hiking the Havasupai Falls near the Grand Canyon?
Which national park in Indiana protects a stretch of Lake Michigan shoreline and tall sand dunes?
What is the ranger-led tour that takes visitors underground in Carlsbad Caverns?
Which national park in New Mexico features a massive underground cavern with a nightly bat flight?
Which park is the only national park in Kansas?
What is the name of the scenic byway that runs through Shenandoah National Park?
Which national park in Wisconsin protects a cluster of sea caves carved into red sandstone?
What large carnivore was naturally recolonizing Glacier National Park from Canada in the 1980s?
Which national park in Kentucky protects the world's longest known cave system?
What is the most common big-game animal found in Rocky Mountain National Park?
Which national park in California contains both the tallest trees on Earth (coast redwoods) and large Roosevelt elk?
What is the name of the famous backcountry permit route in Zion National Park that requires wading chest-deep through water?
Which national park contains the Double Arch and Landscape Arch, which is considered the longest natural arch in the world?
What is the name of the iconic overlook at the South Rim of the Grand Canyon near the park's visitor center?
Which national park in Colorado and New Mexico preserves the volcanic landscape of the Jemez Mountains?
What wading bird with a distinctive pink color is an iconic resident of Everglades National Park?
Which national park in Wyoming, just south of Yellowstone, is known for its dramatic mountain peaks reflected in the Snake River?
What is the name of the permit required to hike to the top of Half Dome in Yosemite?
Which national park in South Carolina and Georgia protects a chain of barrier islands with wild horses?
What is the name of the famous drive in Joshua Tree National Park that passes through both the Mojave and Sonoran Deserts?
medium.a: Park Boulevard (Keys View Road is the most famous viewpoint)

hard.q: What was the original Native American name for the area that became Yellowstone, used by the Crow people?
hard.a: Mitsi a-da-zi (Land of the Burning Ground / Yellow Rock River)

hard.q: Which national park was established in 1890 on the same day as Sequoia National Park, making them among the oldest?
What is the name of the geologist who first formally documented and named the Grand Canyon in an 1869 river expedition?
Which national park in American Samoa protects tropical rain forest and coral reefs in the South Pacific?
What is the name of the volcanic crater at the summit of Haleakalā in Maui that gives that national park its name?
Which national park in the Florida Keys protects the only living coral reef in the continental United States?
What is the name of the massive underground chamber in Carlsbad Caverns that is the size of 14 football fields?
Which national park in Michigan, accessible only by ferry or floatplane, is known for its wolf and moose predator-prey study?
What is the highest peak in the contiguous United States, found in a national park in California?
hard.a: Mount Whitney (14,505 ft) in Sequoia National Park

hard.q: Which national park in the Florida Keys was established in 1992 and contains Fort Jefferson, a 19th-century coastal fortress?
What year was the National Park Service Organic Act signed into law, creating the NPS?
Which explorer and naturalist is credited with lobbying most strongly for the creation of Yosemite as a protected space?
What is the name of the long-distance trail that traverses the full length of the Great Smoky Mountains National Park from east to west?
hard.a: Appalachian Trail (along the ridge) / the 71-mile Smokies section

hard.q: In which national park is Kolob Arch, sometimes cited as one of the world's largest natural arches?
What is the name of the rare and endemic species of trout found only in Yellowstone Lake?
Which national park in Alaska contains the tallest coastal mountains on Earth as well as the Bagley Icefield?
What is the name of the massive caldera that underlies most of Yellowstone National Park?
hard.a: Yellowstone Caldera (also called the Yellowstone Supervolcano)

hard.q: Which national park in North Carolina and Tennessee was the first eastern national park created primarily from privately purchased land?
What is the Organic Act clause that mandates parks be managed to leave them 'unimpaired for the enjoyment of future generations'?
Which national park in Utah is the least visited of the 'Mighty Five' Utah parks and features narrow slot canyons called 'fins'?
What is the name of the 1864 land grant signed by Abraham Lincoln that set aside Yosemite Valley for public use — predating the NPS?
Which national park in the Caribbean protects bioluminescent Mosquito Bay, one of the world's brightest bioluminescent bays?
hard.a: There is no NPS unit there — Mosquito Bay is on Vieques, Puerto Rico (a NWR)

hard.q: What specific rock type, formed from ancient sea-floor sediments, makes up the colorful layers visible in the Grand Canyon walls?
hard.a: Limestone, shale, sandstone, and schist — the oldest is Vishnu Basement Rocks (schist and granite)

hard.q: Which national park in Idaho contains Craters of the Moon, a vast landscape of lava flows, cinder cones, and lava tube caves?
hard.a: Craters of the Moon National Monument and Preserve

hard.q: What is the name of the only road through Death Valley that connects the park from north to south?
Which national park in Missouri protects the site of the first westward gateway arch, designed by Eero Saarinen?
What is the name of the rare night-blooming cactus found in Saguaro National Park?
hard.a: Saguaro (the saguaro cactus blooms at night)

hard.q: What 1980 act of Congress added over 47 million acres to the National Park System in Alaska, creating or expanding parks such as Kenai Fjords and Kobuk Valley?
hard.a: Alaska National Interest Lands Conservation Act (ANILCA)

hard.q: What is the name of the hot spring in Yellowstone that is considered the largest in the United States?
Which national park in Maine was the first national park established east of the Mississippi River?
What is the name of the Ancestral Puebloan great house in Chaco Culture National Historical Park that has over 700 rooms?
Which national park in the Pacific Northwest was established to protect old-growth temperate rain forest and is part of the UNESCO world heritage site?
What is the highest point in the entire National Park System?
Which national park in California contains the world's largest grove of giant sequoias, known as the Giant Forest?
What is the name of the massive granite monolith in Yosemite that is the world's largest exposed granite monolith?
Which national park near Barrow, Alaska lies entirely above the Arctic Circle?
What is the name of the marine terrace accessible only by kayak in Channel Islands National Park that is home to sea lion rookeries?
Which national park in Texas has a higher peak than any mountain east of the Rockies?
What is the name of the abandoned mining town within Death Valley National Park that is open for visitors?
hard.a: Rhyolite (just outside the park) / Leadfield and Skidoo are within the park

hard.q: Which national park protects the largest remaining old-growth bottomland hardwood forest in the southeastern United States?
What is the name of the 150-million-year-old dinosaur tracksite located within Dinosaur National Monument?
hard.a: Carnegie Quarry (the Quarry Exhibit Hall contains over 1,500 bones in situ)

hard.q: Which national park in Virginia follows the crest of the Blue Ridge Mountains for 105 miles and contains over 500 miles of hiking trails?
What is the name of the high-altitude lake above 11,000 feet in Rocky Mountain National Park near Trail Ridge Road?
hard.a: Forest Canyon Overlook area — the lake is Loch Vale / Bear Lake is at 9,475 ft

hard.q: Which national monument in Utah, frequently confused with a national park, contains the Zebra Slot Canyon and Buckskin Gulch?
What is the name of the Hopi-speaking people whose ancestral lands are protected within Grand Canyon National Park?
hard.a: Hopi (and also Navajo, Havasupai, Hualapai, Zuni, and other tribes)

hard.q: Which national park in Tennessee and North Carolina protects the highest point east of the Mississippi River, Clingmans Dome, at 6,643 feet?
What is the name of the endemic fox species found only on the Channel Islands?
Which national park in Wyoming was nearly eliminated from the park system in the 1940s by proposed dam projects on the Snake River?
What is the average annual snowfall at the summit of Mount Rainier, making it one of the snowiest places in the world?
Which national park contains Enchanted Valley, a glacially carved valley sometimes called 'Valley of 10,000 Waterfalls'?
What legislation passed in 1964 created the legal framework for wilderness areas within national parks?
Which national park in New Mexico preserves deep underground cave formations including the world's largest known cave room, the Lechuguilla Cave?
hard.a: Carlsbad Caverns (Lechuguilla Cave is within the park but not open to the public)

hard.q: What is the name of the ancient bristlecone pine tree in Great Basin National Park once identified as the world's oldest living tree?
hard.a: Prometheus (cut down in 1964 at approximately 4,862 years old)

hard.q: Which national park on the coast of Maine has the first sunrise in the continental United States each morning due to its eastern location?
What was the original name of Denali National Park before Congress renamed it in 1980, reflecting the peak's name given by prospector William Dickey in 1897?
